items.title: Shared Alloro email shell uses the approved brand system
items.precondition: Dependencies are available; no database or outbound email connection is required.
steps: Confirm the shared-shell assertions cover the logo, responsive canvas, serif heading stack, sans body stack, monospace code stack, orange CTA, and escaping.
items.expected: The semantic shell checks pass and no in-scope output contains the legacy blue CTA.
items.evidence: Post-rebase focused Vitest run: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed. Strict Code Constitution check, TypeScript, and build also passed.
items.notes: Assertions confirm the shared logo/canvas, approved font stacks, orange #d66853 CTA, escaped helpers, and absence of #2563eb.

items.title: Account and authentication variants render consistently
items.precondition: Use only the synthetic account, organization, codes, and URLs defined by the focused test suite.
steps: Render invitation, verification, password-reset, and temporary-password variants.
Confirm initial and resend paths use the same builders and hostile dynamic values are escaped.
items.expected: Every account/auth variant uses the shared shell, keeps its existing behavioral copy, and safely renders dynamic content.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/email-template-branding.test.ts rendered invitation, verification, reset, and temporary-password variants with normal and hostile synthetic values.
items.notes: Initial/resend invitation and initial/resend verification call the same pure builders; code expiry text and destination URLs were preserved.

items.title: Billing and transport-test variants use the shared shell
items.precondition: Use synthetic organization, location, transport, recipient, quantity, and price values.
steps: Render quantity increase/decrease, all lifecycle states, and the transport-test message.
Confirm totals and lifecycle wording remain correct and dynamic values are escaped.
items.expected: All variants pass semantic rendering assertions without changing billing calculations or transport-send behavior.
items.evidence: Focused tests rendered quantity changes, all four location lifecycle states, and the transport-test email; all assertions passed.
items.notes: Billing calculations, subjects, recipients, lifecycle wording, category, and allowLiveSend behavior remain in their existing send paths.

items.title: User notifications format and escape metadata safely
items.precondition: Use synthetic notification data containing strings, numbers, booleans, dates, nulls, and hostile HTML.
steps: Confirm the title uses the display font, metadata remains readable, and no injected markup survives.
items.expected: All notification variants pass and the metadata contract uses unknown instead of any.
items.evidence: Focused tests rendered all five notification types with hostile title/message/key/value data plus number, boolean, date, and null metadata.
items.notes: Metadata now uses Record<string, unknown>; injected markup is escaped and action behavior is unchanged.

items.title: Representative emails remain readable at narrow and desktop widths
items.precondition: Local Playwright renders synthetic HTML directly; no live send or production data is used.
steps: Render representative invitation, verification-code, billing, notification, transport-test, support, and checkup HTML.
Capture and inspect narrow-width and desktop-width screenshots.
Check for horizontal overflow, clipped content, inconsistent typography, unreadable codes, or blue CTAs.
items.expected: The layout remains readable and Alloro-consistent at both widths with serif headings, sans body copy, orange CTAs, and intentional fallbacks.
items.evidence: 14 screenshots under plans/07162026-transactional-email-brand-consistency/evidence/: invitation, verification, billing, notification, transport-test, support, and checkup at 390px and 760px.
items.notes: Playwright reported overflow=false for every capture. Visual inspection confirmed readable serif headings, sans body copy, monospace codes, orange CTAs, intact cards, and no clipping.

items.title: Controlled inbox QA is routed only to Dave
items.precondition: The sender machine must not be recognized as production, Mailgun must be configured, and the interceptor recipient must equal dave@getalloro.com.
steps: Build all 11 in-scope account, authentication, billing, notification, and transport-test variants with synthetic data and reserved .invalid original recipients.
Run every payload through the email interceptor and assert the final To list contains only dave@getalloro.com while CC and BCC are empty.
Submit each asserted-safe payload directly to Mailgun without invoking account, billing, database, or production-recipient workflows.
items.expected: Mailgun accepts all 11 messages for dave@getalloro.com and no customer or production recipient is addressed.
items.evidence: Preflight: liveSender=false, interceptRecipient=dave@getalloro.com, database host classified local. Mailgun returned HTTP 200 with a message ID for 11 of 11 variants on 2026-07-16.
items.notes: Mailgun acceptance proves the messages were queued, not final inbox placement. Original To, CC, and BCC values used reserved .invalid addresses; the direct transport path avoided product workflows and email-log database writes.
